name: Colognian (Kölsch)

sources.author: Wrede, Adam
sources.title: Neuer Kölnischer Sprachschatz
sources.notes: Standard Kölsch dictionary; a starting point for a future orthography map.

notes: Kölsch (Ripoarisch) — the Ripuarian (Central Franconian, West Germanic) dialect of Cologne. THE GRAPHEME INVENTORY IS NOT YET SPECIFIED here (graphemes/allophones are empty). Kölsch has no single official orthography: writers use ad-hoc German-based spellings, and the two competing conventions (Akademie för uns Kölsche Sproch's 'Rheinische Dokumenta' vs. traditional German-style spelling) map the same letters to different sounds. It also carries a phonemic tone accent (Rhenish Accent 1/2) that is not written at all, so a letter→IPA table would be underdetermined without first committing to one written norm.
